title: taraxacum subrubescens
intro: Taraxacum subrubescens, also known as the western hawk's-beard or a species of dandelion, is a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is primarily found in western North America, where it grows in open fields, meadows, and along roadsides. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis, kidney stones, and digestive issues. Taraxacum subrubescens can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting urinary health.
